title: A multimodal large language model for materials science
excerpt: Nature Machine Intelligence, Published online: 24 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s42256-026-01214-y Tang et al. introduce MatterChat, a multimodal framework effectively integrating material structural data with large language models. It achieves high-precision property predictions and provides interpretable reasoning to accelerate materials discovery.

title: Programmable RNA translation through deep learning-driven IRES discovery and de novo generation
excerpt: Nature Machine Intelligence, Published online: 24 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s42256-026-01213-z Chu et al. present a framework for programmable RNA translation, of interest for RNA therapeutics. The method enables large-scale discovery and engineering of internal ribosome entry sites, which are validated by high-throughput functional assays.

title: Dxdirector: an agentic large language model driving the full-process clinical diagnosis
excerpt: Authors develop an autonomous agentic LLM called DxDirector-7B. Its clinical diagnostic accuracy outperforms larger models. Using deep thinking strategies, it significantly reduces physician workload, requesting human assistance only when necessary.

title: FDA Approves First-Ever Gene Therapy for Treatment of Genetic Hearing Loss Under National Priority Voucher Program
excerpt: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today approved Otarmeni (lunsotogene parvec-cwha), the first-ever dual adeno-associated virus (AAV) vector-based gene therapy.

title: CMS and FDA Announce RAPID Coverage Pathway to Accelerate Patient Access to Life-Changing Medical Devices
excerpt: CMS and FDA announce the Regulatory Alignment for Predictable and Immediate Device (RAPID) coverage pathway, a new pathway designed to expedite access to certain FDA-designated Class II and Class III Breakthrough Devices for people with Medicare.

title: Algebraic language models for inverse design of metamaterials via diffusion transformers
excerpt: Nature Machine Intelligence, Published online: 23 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s42256-026-01218-8 Architected materials are ubiquitous, yet their design remains constrained by complex structure–property relationships. title: Molecular deep learning at the edge of chemical space
excerpt: Nature Machine Intelligence, Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s42256-026-01216-w van Tilborg et al. introduce a deep learning model trained to simultaneously predict bioactivity and reconstruct molecules. The model provides a principled measure of distribution shifts, experimentally validated by discovering active inhibitors for two clinically relevant kinase targets.
